question.text: How is dividend (IDCW) income from mutual funds taxed in the hands of investors?

answer.text: It is completely tax-free for the investor
At a flat rate of 10% for all investors
At the investor's income tax slab rate

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Since April 2020, dividend (now called IDCW - Income Distribution cum Capital Withdrawal) from mutual funds is taxed in the hands of the investor at their income tax slab rate. The mutual fund no longer pays Dividend Distribution Tax (DDT). The investor must declare this income in their tax return. **The Trap:** Before April 2020, dividends were tax-free in investor hands as the fund paid DDT. This changed significantly. Also, TDS of 10% is deducted if annual dividend exceeds Rs. 5,000 from a single fund house. **Cross-Exam Context:** Also tested in V-A and Series XXIV. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** For retired investors in higher tax brackets, the IDCW option may not be tax-efficient. Growth option with systematic withdrawals (SWP) could be more tax-friendly depending on their situation.
